title: lasianthera littoralis
intro: Lasianthera littoralis, also known as Wild Jasmine or Coast Jasmine, is a flowering evergreen shrub native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Lasianthera littoralis include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as infusions and topical applications, to harness its medicinal properties.
